rule.name: AWS ECR Container Upload Unknown User
rule.description: The following analytic detects the upload of a new container image to AWS Elastic Container Registry (ECR) by an unknown user. It leverages AWS CloudTrail logs to identify `PutImage` events from the ECR service, filtering out known users. This activity is significant because container uploads should typically be performed by a limited set of authorized users. If confirmed malicious, this could indicate unauthorized access, potentially leading to the deployment of malicious containers, data exfiltration, or further compromise of the AWS environment.
rule.query:
`cloudtrail` eventSource=ecr.amazonaws.com eventName=PutImage NOT `aws_ecr_users`
  | rename requestParameters.* as *
  | rename repositoryName AS image
  | rename user_name as user
  | stats count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime
    BY signature user user_agent
       src vendor_account vendor_region
       vendor_product image
  | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
  | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
  | `aws_ecr_container_upload_unknown_user_filter`
false_positives: No false positives have been identified at this time.
